# PlaceMux — Task 23: MLOps Foundations — Feature Store & Model Registry (AI/ML Engineer, Week 6 / Phase 3)

A production-grade MLOps foundation for PlaceMux's student↔job matching system, built to Task 23's study guide and scoring rubric.

**This has been executed end-to-end** — real numbers are in `experiments/final_report.json` and `experiments/metrics_report.md`, generated by `run_pipeline.py` across 6 simulated months. All models are tracked in a **Model Registry**, and all features are served through a **Feature Store**.

---

## What's New in Task 23 (vs Task 22)

| Task 22 | Task 23 |
|---------|---------|
| Features computed ad-hoc per request | **Feature Store** (`src/feature_store.py`) — centralized entity lookup, online inference + historical joins |
| Models saved as loose `.joblib` files | **Model Registry** (`src/registry.py`) — versioned catalog, metrics logging, Production promotion |
| API required full nested skill dicts | API queries Feature Store via `student_id` + `job_id` only |
| No drift detection | Still inherits full PSI drift monitoring + auto-retraining |

---

## Folder structure

```
week6-task23/
├── data/
│   ├── generate_data.py                 # generates 6 months of real-shaped synthetic data
│   ├── students.csv                     # 1,200 students, verified skill scores
│   ├── jobs.csv                         # 258 JDs, weighted required skills
│   └── interactions_2026-0X.csv         # 1,500 labeled pairs per month (9,000 total)
├── src/
│   ├── feature_store.py                 # [NEW] Feature Store: online + historical feature retrieval
│   ├── registry.py                      # [NEW] Model Registry: log, version, promote to Production
│   ├── features.py                      # Feature space definition + input validation
│   ├── baseline.py                      # Dumb overlap-ratio baseline
│   ├── model.py                         # GradientBoosting match model (fit/predict/save/load)
│   ├── metrics.py                       # precision / recall / FPR / ROC-AUC / segment breakdown
│   ├── drift_detector.py                # PSI-based feature & prediction drift detection
│   └── retrain_pipeline.py              # Monitoring loop using Feature Store + Registry
├── api/
│   └── serve.py                         # FastAPI /match — queries Feature Store, uses Registry
├── demo/
│   └── demo_walkthrough.py              # One real student, one real job, live score + why
├── tests/
│   ├── test_pipeline.py                 # 9 edge-case tests (all passing)
│   └── test_mlops.py                    # [NEW] Feature Store & Registry unit tests (all passing)
├── experiments/
│   ├── final_report.json                # Full machine-readable results
│   └── metrics_report.md                # Human-readable metrics report
├── models/
│   ├── model_v1.joblib, model_v2.joblib  # Every trained version, kept
│   ├── model_latest.joblib               # Hardcoded fallback for the API
│   └── registry_metadata.json           # [NEW] Registry catalog (versions, metrics, status)
├── run_pipeline.py                       # Top-level orchestrator — run this
└── requirements.txt
```

---

## How to run it yourself

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python3 data/generate_data.py     # regenerate 6 months of data (optional, already included)
python3 run_pipeline.py           # initialises Feature Store + Registry, runs drift/retrain pipeline
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v       # 9 existing + 2 new MLOps-specific tests (11 total)
python3 demo/demo_walkthrough.py  # one real example, live, end-to-end
uvicorn api.serve:app --reload    # serve /match for real inference using Feature Store + Registry
```

---

## Feature Store

`src/feature_store.py` provides two modes:

### Online Mode (for inference)
```python
fs = FeatureStore("data/")
feat = fs.get_online_features("S001", "J042")
# -> {"overlap_count": 3, "overlap_ratio": 0.75, "weighted_skill_score": 0.82, ...}
```
The API (`api/serve.py`) uses this — callers now send only `student_id` + `job_id`. The feature store looks up the entity profiles and computes features in ~1ms, without requiring the caller to send the full nested skill dictionaries.

### Historical Mode (for training)
```python
feats_df = fs.get_historical_features(interactions_df)
```
Used by the training pipeline — performs a point-in-time join on the interaction log with entity profiles.

---

## Model Registry

`src/registry.py` provides a versioned model catalog persisted in `models/registry_metadata.json`:

```python
registry = ModelRegistry()
registry.log_model(model, "v2", {"precision": 0.649, "recall": 0.568, ...})
registry.promote_to_production("v2")
```

The API dynamically fetches the `Production`-tagged model path from the registry on first request. Promotion copies the model to `model_latest.joblib` for hardcoded failover too.

### Registry State (from real run)

```json
{
  "models": {
    "v1": {"status": "Archived", "metrics": {"precision": 0.641, "recall": 0.327, ...}},
    "v2": {"status": "Production", "metrics": {"precision": 0.649, "recall": 0.568, ...}}
  },
  "production_version": "v2"
}
```

---

## Real Numbers from Last Run

**Baseline vs Trained Model (held-out month 2026-03):**

| Metric | Baseline (skill-overlap) | Trained model |
|---|---|---|
| Precision | 0.324 | 0.444 |
| Recall | 0.740 | 0.410 |
| False Positive Rate | 0.451 | **0.150** |
| ROC AUC | 0.708 | 0.771 |

**Drift monitoring + Registry timeline:**

| Month | Drift status | Max PSI | Event | Model | Precision | Recall |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jan–Mar (train) | — | — | initial_train | v1 | 0.641 | 0.327 |
| Apr | MODERATE_DRIFT | 0.246 | monitor_only | v1 | 0.438 | 0.209 |
| May | MODERATE_DRIFT | 0.249 | monitor_only | v1 | 0.460 | 0.226 |
| Jun | **SIGNIFICANT_DRIFT** | 0.475 | **drift_triggered_retrain → v2** | **v2** | **0.649** | **0.568** |

---

## Mapping to the Scoring Rubric

**Feature Store (25 pts)** — `src/feature_store.py`: both online (API) and historical (training) feature retrieval implemented. API now requires only `student_id` + `job_id` — no client-side feature engineering needed.

**Model Registry (25 pts)** — `src/registry.py`: every model run logged with precision/recall/FPR, promotion to `Production` triggers a copy to `model_latest.joblib` as failover. Registry state is persisted in `models/registry_metadata.json`.

**Pipeline integration (20 pts)** — `run_pipeline.py` orchestrates Feature Store initialization, training via `get_historical_features`, drift monitoring, model registration, and `promote_to_production`.

**Tests & edge-case handling (15 pts)** — 11 tests total, all passing: 9 original edge-case tests + 2 new MLOps-specific tests for Feature Store and Registry.

**API & live serving (15 pts)** — `api/serve.py` queries Registry for the Production model path on startup, falls back to `model_latest.joblib` if registry metadata is missing.

---

## API Contract

**Simplified (Task 23) vs old (Task 22):**

```bash
# Old (Task 22) - required full skill dictionaries
POST /match
{"student_id": "S001", "job_id": "J042",
 "student_skills": {"python": 0.8, "sql": 0.7, ...},
 "job_skills": {"python": 0.9, ...}, "years_gap": 1}

# New (Task 23) - only IDs needed
POST /match
{"student_id": "S001", "job_id": "J042"}
```

Response includes `score`, `decision`, `model_version`, and `explanation`.

---

## Definition of Done — Self-Check

- [x] Feature Store built and integrated — online + historical modes, used in both API and pipeline.
- [x] Model Registry built and integrated — every model version logged with metrics, Production promotion live.
- [x] Pipeline uses Registry and Feature Store end-to-end — not just written, executed with real numbers above.
- [x] API simplified — callers send IDs only, Feature Store does the join.
- [x] Tests passing — 11/11 tests, including dedicated Feature Store and Registry tests.
- [x] Registry metadata persisted — `models/registry_metadata.json` survives restarts.

---

## Known Limitations

- Feature Store is file-backed (CSV), not a streaming/real-time store (e.g. Feast, Tecton). Sufficient for this scale; would need a time-travel-aware store in production for true point-in-time correctness.
- Registry metadata is JSON; no locking for concurrent writers. Fine for a single-pipeline setup; a production registry would use a database with row-level locking.
- Drift is checked once per monthly batch; a production system would checkpoint on a rolling daily window.
